front: What does 'terraform init' do and when must you run it?
back:
terraform init initializes a working directory containing Terraform configuration files. It is always the first command you run.

What it does:
1. Downloads provider plugins (aws, snowflake, databricks, etc.)
2. Downloads module sources (registry, git, local)
3. Sets up the backend (configures where state is stored)
4. Creates .terraform/ directory (do NOT commit this)
5. Creates .terraform.lock.hcl (DO commit this for reproducible builds)

When to re-run:
- First time cloning a repo
- After adding/changing a provider
- After adding/changing a module source
- After changing backend configuration
- After deleting the .terraform/ directory

Useful flags:
```bash
terraform init -upgrade        # upgrade providers to latest allowed version
terraform init -reconfigure    # force backend reconfiguration
terraform init -backend=false  # skip backend init (useful in CI for validation only)
```

.gitignore for Terraform repos:
```
.terraform/
terraform.tfstate
terraform.tfstate.backup
*.tfvars (if they contain secrets)
```
